name: Dagestan (Russia)

colors.symbolism: life, abundance, and Islam, the predominant faith of the peoples of Dagestan
the Caspian Sea, the sky, and the beauty of the country
courage, valour, and the revolutionary heritage of the peoples of Dagestan

description: A horizontal triband of green, blue and red of equal width.
history: Adopted on 26 February 2003 by the People's Assembly of the Republic of Dagestan, replacing a 1994 version with slightly different proportions and shades. The blue stripe was originally specified as a sky-blue tone.

features.symbolism: green stands for life, abundance and Islam; blue (or sometimes a lighter shade) for the Caspian Sea and the beauty of Dagestan; red for the courage, valour and revolutionary heritage of the people of Dagestan.
